title: North Korea / DPRK Tracker Update — Apr 29, 2026
summary: Kim Jong-un inaugurated the 'Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations' on April 27, marking the first public acknowledgment of DPRK soldiers killed in Ukraine and praising those who died rather than surrender. Russian Defense Minister Belousov visited Pyongyang the same day and agreed with Defense Minister No Kwang Chol on a 2027–2031 Military Cooperation Plan, formalizing the deepest Russia-DPRK alliance since the Cold War. South Korean NIS estimates ~15,000 DPRK troops were deployed to Kursk, with ~2,000 killed — significantly higher than earlier estimates. On April 28, a New Zealand P-8A Poseidon aircraft observed a suspected ship-to-ship DPRK sanctions transfer in the Yellow Sea. Earlier that week, Kim supervised Hwasong-11 Ra tactical missile tests with cluster munitions on April 20, the fourth launch in April and seventh of 2026.

title: North Korea / DPRK Tracker Update — Tue Apr 28, 2026
summary: New Zealand P-8A Poseidon Observes Possible DPRK Ship-to-Ship Sanctions Transfer.

title: North Korea / DPRK Tracker Update — Mon Apr 27, 2026
summary: Kim Jong-un Opens Memorial Museum for DPRK Soldiers Killed in Ukraine. Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov traveled to Pyongyang and met with Kim Jong-un and North Korean Defense Minister No Kwang Chol.

title: Tracker initialized
summary: Initial backfill complete. 80 Years of the Kim Dynasty: DPRK Deploys Troops to Russia, Ends Reunification Goal

title: North Korea / DPRK Tracker Update — Mon Apr 20, 2026
summary: Kim Jong-un Supervises Hwasong-11 Ra Cluster Munition Missile Tests with Daughter.
